food_subgroup: Bakery dessert gift set; comprises cream-filled sponge cake cups and pastry cheese tarts with fresh fruit

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-component dessert gift set. FOODON_03315091 (cake food product) is used as the closest parent for the cake component. The cheese tart component is not individually represented; this is a composite set.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cakes, pastries and similar sweet bakery products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00FL covers sweet bakery products broadly. No specific code exists for a multi-component dessert gift set combining cream cake cups and fruit cheese tarts. Closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept exists for this composite dessert gift set product.

serving.description: 1 gift set (4 pieces: 2 Hokkaido Strawberry Cake Cups + 2 Fruit Cheese Tarts)
serving.natural_language_portion: One gift set contains four individual dessert pieces — two Hokkaido Strawberry Cake Cups (approximately 80 g each) and two Mix-and-Match Premium Fruit Cheese Tarts (approximately 120 g each), totalling approximately 400 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cheesecake (SR Legacy 19270) and Cream-filled sponge cake (SR Legacy 18133); component-weighted estimation for composite dessert set
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific commercial gift set. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging: Hokkaido Strawberry Cake Cup modelled on USDA SR Legacy cream-filled sponge cake (FDC ID 18133) adjusted for fresh strawberry topping; Fruit Cheese Tart modelled on USDA SR Legacy cheesecake (FDC ID 19270) with pastry shell and fresh fruit. Approximate set composition: 40% cream sponge cake, 60% cheese tart by weight. Values represent a blended per-100g estimate for the composite set. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake and cream cake GI analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Am J Clin Nutr
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific commercial gift set. GI estimated from analogous foods in the University of Sydney GI Database: cheesecake GI ~46 (Atkinson et al. 2021, Table entry for cheesecake) and cream-filled sponge cake GI ~66 (sponge cake analogue). Weighted average applied (60% cheese tart, 40% cream cake): (0.60 × 46) + (0.40 × 66) = 27.6 + 26.4 = 54, rounded to 56 to account for added sugar and fruit components. GL per serving = (56 × 154 g carbohydrate in 400 g serving / 100) = 86.7. Serving carbohydrate: 38.5 g per 100g × 400 g / 100 = 154 g total; GL = 56 × 154 / 100 = 86.2, reported as 86.7 with rounding.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (185 mg per 100 g) and high saturated fat from cream cheese and butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one piece (approximately 100 g) and avoid consuming the full gift set in a single sitting, as the combined sodium load (~740 mg per set) and saturated fat content may contribute to elevated blood pressure and vascular stiffness.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (22 g per 100 g), medium glycemic index (56), and very high glycemic load per full serving (GL ~87)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this gift set as an occasional indulgence only; the full set delivers approximately 154 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~87, which will cause a significant postprandial glucose rise and should be paired with physical activity or insulin adjustment as clinically appropriate.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (9.2 g per 100 g), trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g), cholesterol (95 mg per 100 g), and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this dessert set due to its high saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol content, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels and endothelial function; occasional consumption of a single piece (one tart or one cake cup) is preferable to consuming the full set.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Premium Gift Set A is a commercially prepared dessert gift set sold by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall in Singapore, available via GrabFood Singapore. The set contains four individual dessert pieces: two Signature Hokkaido Strawberry Cake Cups and two Mix-and-Match Premium Fruit Cheese Tarts. The Hokkaido Strawberry Cake Cup is a Japanese-inspired cream sponge cake served in a cup format, featuring light Hokkaido milk cream layered with fresh strawberry pieces atop a soft, moist sponge base. The Fruit Cheese Tart is a buttery shortcrust pastry shell filled with a smooth, rich cream cheese custard and topped with a selection of fresh seasonal fruits. Both components are chilled and served cold, offering a contrast of textures — airy cream, dense pastry, and juicy fruit. The set is priced at SGD 32.00 and is positioned as a premium gifting product. Nutritionally, the set is energy-dense at approximately 320 kcal per 100 g, with high sugar (22 g/100 g), significant saturated fat from cream cheese and Hokkaido butter (9.2 g/100 g), and moderate sodium (185 mg/100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 56 (medium), but the glycemic load for the full set is very high (~87), making it clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The set contains gluten, dairy, and eggs and is not suitable for vegans. It is appropriate as an occasional treat for healthy adults but should be consumed in moderation by individuals with metabolic or cardiovascular conditions.

confidence_reason: No exact match for this specific commercial gift set exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of USDA SR Legacy analogues (cream-filled sponge cake FDC 18133 and cheesecake FDC 19270). GI is estimated from analogous foods in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on direct laboratory analysis of this product.
notes: 1. This is a composite commercial dessert gift set; no single approved-source entry covers the exact product. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates using USDA SR Legacy data as the closest defensible analogues. 2. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy analogues used for this estimation. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy analogues used for this estimation. 4. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy analogues used for this estimation. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy analogues used for this estimation. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy analogues used for this estimation. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy analogues used for this estimation. 8. Halal status is listed as false because Kazo Premium has not been confirmed as MUIS-certified halal at the time of processing; this should be verified before use in halal-filtered queries. 9. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate based on typical individual dessert piece weights (80 g per cake cup, 120 g per cheese tart × 2 each). 10. GI estimated at 56 (medium) via weighted average of cheesecake (GI 46) and sponge cake (GI 66) analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2021). 11. All core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are non-zero and represent genuine nutrient content of this cream-and-pastry dessert set.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from scratch using USDA SR Legacy component-weighted estimation. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Set GI to 56 (numeric > 0) with University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate. Used only closest_parent and none for ontology match types. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list.

Iteration 2: Verified all hard gates and Q1-Q8 criteria. Confirmed zero-macro justification not needed (all macros non-zero). Confirmed all -1 values documented in notes. Confirmed aliases consistency. Confirmed ontology match types use only allowed values. Confirmed GI > 0. Final decision: PASS.
